articles.title: ZAYA1-8B Technical Report
articles.summary: ZAYA1-8B is a reasoning-focused mixture-of-experts model with 700M active and 8B total parameters, built on Zyphra's MoE++ architecture. With under 1B active parameters, it matches or exceeds DeepSeek-R1-0528 on several challenging mathematics and coding benchmarks. The model was trained on a full-stack AMD compute platform.

articles.title: Leviathan: Decoupling Input and Output Representations in Language Models
articles.summary: Leviathan introduces a Transformer architecture that replaces the input embedding matrix with learned embedding vectorization (LEV), a compact continuous mapping from token indices to embeddings. This decouples token representation from vocabulary discrimination objectives. The approach addresses limitations of tied embedding matrices in modern language models.

articles.title: Continuous Latent Diffusion Language Model
articles.summary: Cola DLM proposes a hierarchical latent diffusion language model that frames text generation through hierarchical information structuring rather than fixed left-to-right order. The model aims to achieve generation efficiency, scalable representation learning, and effective global semantic modeling beyond traditional autoregressive paradigms.

articles.title: Improving token efficiency in GitHub Agentic Workflows
articles.summary: GitHub analyzes how agentic workflows running on every pull request can quietly accumulate large API bills. They share how they instrumented production workflows, identified inefficiencies, and built agents to optimize token usage while maintaining functionality.

articles.title: Agent pull requests are everywhere. Here's how to review them.
articles.summary: A practical guide for reviewing agent-generated pull requests, covering what to look for, where issues commonly hide, and how to catch technical debt before it ships. The guide addresses the growing prevalence of AI-generated code contributions in software development workflows.

articles.title: Litespark Inference on Consumer CPUs: Custom SIMD Kernels for Ternary Neural Networks
articles.summary: Introduces custom SIMD kernels that enable ternary neural networks to run efficiently on consumer CPUs, making LLM inference accessible without expensive datacenter GPUs. The approach uses weights constrained to {-1, 0, +1} to theoretically eliminate floating-point multiplication while achieving practical speedups.

articles.title: Emergent Slow Thinking in LLMs as Inverse Tree Freezing
articles.summary: Provides a statistical-physics explanation for how reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards enables slow, multi-step reasoning in LLMs. The paper shows that finite model capacity forces compression of exponentially large prefix spaces into Markov networks where reasoning unfolds as random walks through concept networks.

articles.title: Data Language Models: A New Foundation Model Class for Tabular Data
articles.summary: Introduces a new class of foundation models that understand tabular data natively without preprocessing pipelines. Unlike existing approaches that require complex data transformation, these models can consume raw tabular data directly, potentially revolutionizing how AI handles structured data.

articles.title: Understanding Annotator Safety Policy with Interpretability
articles.summary: Analyzes disagreement in safety policy annotation, distinguishing between operational failures, policy ambiguity, and value pluralism. The research provides insights into why human annotators disagree on safety assessments and how to improve AI safety evaluation processes.

articles.title: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Financial Document Question Answering
articles.summary: Proposes FinAgent-RAG, an agentic framework for financial document QA that handles complex multi-step numerical reasoning over heterogeneous evidence. The system addresses limitations of single-pass retrieve-then-generate approaches in financial analysis scenarios requiring compositional reasoning chains.

articles.title: BALAR: A Bayesian Agentic Loop for Active Reasoning
articles.summary: Introduces a task-agnostic framework that enables LLMs to reason about missing information and determine which questions to ask next in interactive settings. The approach uses Bayesian principles to guide information exchange without requiring fine-tuning for specific tasks.

articles.title: The future of game consoles is looking bleak
articles.summary: Nintendo announced a $50 price increase for the Switch 2, following similar moves by Sony and Microsoft. The piece examines how console gaming faces challenges from rising costs, market saturation, and competition from mobile and PC gaming platforms.

articles.title: Tesla is recalling its cheaper Cybertruck because the wheels might fall off
articles.summary: Tesla recalls all 173 RWD Cybertruck Long Range vehicles due to faulty brake rotors that could cause wheels to separate from the hub. The recall affects the $70,000 model with 18-inch steel wheels, with three warranty claims already identified.

articles.title: Sony's PS5 sales plummet amid price rises and a memory crisis
articles.summary: Sony sold just 1.5 million PS5 consoles in Q4, down 46% year-over-year, following two price increases that pushed the regular PS5 from $499.99 to $649.99. The company blames global economic pressures and an ongoing memory crisis for the price hikes.

articles.title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
articles.summary: OpenAI introduces new voice intelligence capabilities in its API for customer service systems and other applications. The features expand beyond traditional customer service to education and creator platforms, offering more versatile voice AI integration options.

articles.title: Perplexity's Personal Computer is now available to everyone on Mac
articles.summary: Perplexity's Personal Computer feature, which brings AI agents to Mac computers, is now open to all users. The tool represents an expansion of AI agent capabilities to desktop computing environments.

articles.title: llm-gemini 0.31
articles.summary: Simon Willison releases version 0.31 of his llm-gemini plugin, which provides command-line access to Google's Gemini models. This update likely includes new features and improvements for developers using Gemini through the LLM CLI tool.

articles.title: Behind the Scenes Hardening Firefox with Claude Mythos Preview
articles.summary: Simon Willison discusses how Mozilla is using Anthropic's Claude Mythos to improve Firefox security through automated vulnerability discovery. This represents a practical application of AI for browser security hardening.

articles.title: Notes on the xAI/Anthropic data center deal
articles.summary: Simon Willison provides analysis and commentary on the reported data center partnership between xAI and Anthropic. The post likely examines the implications of this collaboration for the AI industry landscape.
